reasoning: The tournament has reached the final, and only two teams remain: Spain and Argentina. Spain is the clear favorite, having entered as the pre-tournament favorite at +450 and reaching the final in the same position, opening at -164 to -175 to lift the trophy, backed by a record-tying 37-game unbeaten streak after beating France 2-0 in the semifinal. Argentina are live underdogs at roughly +125 (about 42% on prediction markets), having repeatedly produced late comebacks—most recently two goals in under 10 minutes to beat England 2-1—with Messi setting up both. The main threat to Spain is exactly that Argentine resilience and big-game pedigree as defending champions, plus Messi's proven ability to elevate in finals; my darkhorse label goes to Morocco as the tournament's most credible outsider run, though they are already eliminated at this stage.
